title: Smaller Things - a look at small games that pack a lot of punch!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aqbo0yu_l6g

Daniel (@dungeondive) does a shelf-tour of the small-box dungeon-crawl and adventure games on his radar at the time — "small box" defined by what fits in a normal backpack, which knocks Shadows of Malice and Pathfinder ACG out and keeps Warhammer Quest ACG and Escape the Dark Castle in.

Highlights from the run-through: *Dungeoneer* (best competitive small-box crawl, four sets on the shelf), Hero Realms with Ruin of Thandar (light deck-builder against AI deck), *Death Angel* (harder than Space Hulk to win), *Samurai Spirit* (Ghost Stories-adjacent, doesn't quite land), *Elder Sign + Gates of Arkham*, Warhammer Quest ACG (preferred to the Terrinoth retheme, lamented for being abandoned), *Iron Helm* and *Rogue Dungeon* from Game Crafter, *Unbroken* ("feels like balancing a spreadsheet," still trying to enjoy it), *House of Danger*, *Dark Venture*, *Legends Untold* (most game per cubic inch on the shelf), *Bag of Dungeon*, *Dungeon of D*, *Spire's End* (reprint incoming), and a quiet revisit of his *Storymaster's Tales: Weirding Wood* review — the one that got roasted because his expectations didn't match the marketing.

Open call to viewers: send small-box recommendations.

----

Which small-box game on this shelf has aged best in the years since this video, and which one would Daniel drop quietly off the list now?
